title: Markdown viewer shows stale embedded local images after file content changes on disk (incl. Refresh file button)
author: fbartho
body: ### Pre-submit Checks - [x] I have searched Warp bugs and there are no duplicates. Closest match is #12802 ("Displayed image is stale when underlying file is changed at the same path"), which was fixed by PR #12840 — but that fix is explicitly scoped to AI-block image display only. The PR description states: "non-blocklist callers (themes, **notebooks**) keep path-only caching (`content_version: None`), so their behavior is unchanged." The standalone Markdown document viewer (`FileNotebookView`, opening a `.md` file from the File Tree / Code Editor) is one of those un-fixed "notebooks" callers, and reproduces the identical bug today on current `master` (`13902088`). Also related: #9789 ("Image cache is not getting cleared", unbounded growth — different symptom, same two caches). - [x] I have searched Warp known issues page and my issue is not there. ### Describe the bug When a Markdown document is opened in the rendered Markdown viewer and it embeds a local image via `![alt](relative/dir/chart.svg)`, regenerating that image file on disk (even via an atomic temp-file-plus-rename replace, i.e. a new inode) does not update the rendered image. I tried every refresh mechanism available in the UI and all four failed: 1. Closing the document pane and reopening the file — stale image persists. 2. Toggling between Rendered and Raw view modes — stale image persists. 3. The pane header overflow menu's **"Refresh file"** action — refreshes the document's text content but not the embedded images. 4. Re-editing/re-saving the Markdown file itself (forces a text reload) — embedded images still stale. Only writing the image to a brand-new file path (and updating the Markdown reference) shows the new content, which points at a cache keyed purely by path. ### Root cause (from reading the open source) Confirmed by reading the checked-out source at `13902088` (current `master`), not just inferred from symptoms. **The cache key never changes when a Markdown-embedded image's file content changes:** - `resolve_asset_source_relative_to_directory()` in `crates/editor/src/content/edit.rs` (lines ~78–100) builds the `AssetSource::LocalFile { path, content_version: None }` for every image referenced from Markdown — both the absolute-path branch (line 87) and the relative-path branch (line 98) hardcode `content_version: None`. - `AssetSource::LocalFile` has an optional `content_version: Option` field (`crates/warpui_core/src/assets/asset_cache.rs:125-135`) that fingerprints mtime + file size specifically to solve this class of bug (doc comment: *"so a file whose contents change on disk is treated as a distinct asset and re-read, rather than served from a now-stale cache entry"*). The helper that populates it, `AssetSource::with_local_file_content_version()` (`asset_cache.rs:144-156`), is called from exactly **one** call site in the whole codebase: `app/src/ai/blocklist/block.rs:1766`, inside AI-block image resolution. The Markdown editor's `resolve_asset_source_relative_to_directory()` never calls it. - Because the cache key (`AssetHandle{ source, asset_type }`) is identical before and after the file changes, `AssetCache::load_asset()` (`asset_cache.rs:410-469`) hits `assets.contains_key(&key)` → `true` on line 419 and returns the already-loaded (stale) entry without ever re-reading the file. - A second cache layer compounds this: `ImageCache::image()` (`crates/warpui_core/src/image_cache.rs:862-966`) additionally caches the *rendered* (resized/rasterized) image, keyed by a `DefaultHasher` hash of the same `AssetSource` plus size/fit options (line 873-875) — so even if the first layer were fixed, this layer needs the same content-version-aware key to avoid serving a stale rasterization. There is an `evict_size()` method (line 836) intended to support targeted eviction, but it is marked `#[allow(dead_code)]` with the comment "Called by the debounce eviction pass added in the main changeset" / `TODO(APP-3877): remove #[allow(dead_code)] once the debounce eviction pass wires this up" — i.e. that eviction path isn't wired to any caller yet. **Why all four refresh attempts fail, specifically:** - Close/reopen and Rendered/Raw toggle: both re-render from the same `AssetSource` derived from the same path, so they hit the same cache key. - **"Refresh file"** (`FileNotebookAction::ReloadFile`, handled by `reload_file()` in `app/src/notebooks/file/mod.rs:570-583`) calls `self.open(path, session, ctx)`, which re-reads the *containing Markdown document's own bytes* from disk — but the embedded image asset sources are resolved independently via `resolve_asset_source_relative_to_directory()`, whose output is cache-key-identical to before. The button's own text/reload path works correctly; only the nested image assets are left stale, which is why it looks like a targeted bug in the button rather than a general cache problem. All four surfaces trace back to the same single defect: the Markdown image resolution path never opts into `content_version`. I'm filing this as one ticket (with "Refresh file" listed as an affected surface) rather than two, since fixing the resolver is a single, shared fix — no separate plumbing exists for the button. ### Proposed fix Mirror what PR #12840 already did for the AI-block path, applied to the Markdown editor's image resolution instead: - In `crates/editor/src/content/edit.rs`, have `resolve_asset_source_relative_to_directory()` (or its caller, off the render hot path per the existing doc comment on `with_local_file_content_version()`) call `.with_local_file_content_version()` before constructing the final `AssetSource::LocalFile`, so `content_version` is populated from mtime + file size instead of hardcoded to `None`. - This should be enough for both cache layers, since `content_version` is part of `AssetSource`, which is the key for both `AssetCache` (decoded bytes) and `ImageCache` (rendered pixels). - Given the existing PR's note that this metadata read is "off the render hot path," care should be taken about where in the Markdown parse/render pipeline the stat call happens so it isn't done per-frame — the AI-block precedent (stat once when the block resolves its image sources: creation / output completion / restore / shell-change) is a reasonable model for "stat once when the document's block list is (re)built, including on explicit Refresh file." - Separately (lower priority, already tracked in #9789/APP-3877): `ImageCache::evict_size()` is unused dead code; wiring it into an actual eviction/debounce pass would also help the unbounded-growth issue, though it's not required to fix the staleness reported here — a changed `content_version` alone makes stale entries unreachable (they'll just accumulate until the existing size-based `AssetCache` eviction or window-resize/size-change eviction reclaims them). I'd frame the specifics of *where* to trigger the stat as a judgment call for whoever picks this up — I don't have full visibility into how block-list resolution timing compares to Markdown's parse/layout pipeline, so the AI-block PR is offered as prior art, not a prescription. Related: #13652 (raw-HTML `` support — same markdown viewer surface; spec PR: #13656) and #13657 (rendered SVG/image viewer panel — filed the same day, would reuse this same image pipeline). ### To reproduce 1. Create a Markdown file that embeds a local image, e.g. `![chart](chart.svg)`, and open it in Warp's Markdown viewer (rendered mode). 2. Confirm the image renders. 3. Overwrite `chart.svg` on disk with different visual content, using an atomic temp-file + rename replace (so the file gets a new inode, ruling out any naive "same inode" caching theory). 4. Try, in turn: closing and reopening the document pane; toggling Rendered ↔ Raw; the pane header overflow menu's "Refresh file" action. 5. **Actual:** in all three cases, the original image is still displayed. 6. **Expected:** the updated image content is displayed after any of these refresh actions — most of all "Refresh file," an explicit user-triggered refresh affordance that currently silently no-ops for images while appearing to work (it does refresh the surrounding text). ### Expected behavior An explicit refresh of a Markdown document (via "Refresh file," reopening the pane, or toggling view mode) should reflect the current on-disk content of every embedded image, not just the document's own text. ### Screenshots, videos, and logs N/A — this is a source-level analysis; happy to attach a screen recording if useful for triage. ### Operating system (OS) macOS ### Operating system and version macOS 27.0 (Darwin 27.0.0) ### Shell Version zsh ### Current Warp version 0.2026.07.01.09.21.01 ### Regression No, this bug or issue has existed throughout my experience using Warp ### Recent working Warp date N/A ### Additional context Source read at commit `13902088` (current `master` as of 2026-07-13; the local checkout's active branch, `fbartho/gh13652-markdown-img-sizing-spec`, is that same commit plus only additive files under `specs/GH13652/`, so all cited source files are identical to `master`). ### Does this block you from using Warp daily? No ### Is this an issue only in Warp? Yes, I confirmed that this only happens in Warp, not other terminals.

title: Rendered SVG/image viewer panel (analogous to the Markdown viewer)
author: fbartho
body: ### Describe the solution you'd like? Opening an `.svg` (or other image) file in Warp currently shows the raw file content — for SVG, that's raw XML text. Warp already has a rendered viewer panel for Markdown files (with a Rendered/Raw toggle), and that Markdown viewer can already display images, including SVGs, inline. Requesting: give SVGs and other common image formats (PNG, JPEG, GIF, WebP) their own rendered image-viewer panel when opened directly, analogous to the Markdown viewer — instead of falling through to a plain-text code editor pane. Basic expectations for a first version: - Opening a `.svg`/`.png`/`.jpg`/`.gif`/`.webp` file (double-click in the file tree, `cat`/`open` in terminal, etc.) opens a rendered image pane instead of raw text/binary garbage. - Minimal chrome: fit-to-pane by default, maybe a zoom control. Doesn't need to be fancy for v1. - A Raw/Rendered toggle similar to what the Markdown viewer offers would be a nice-to-have but isn't essential for v1. ### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe. I generate SVG charts/dashboards locally (dataviz output, diagrams, etc.) and open them directly in Warp to check them. Right now that just dumps the raw XML into a text pane, which is useless — I have to shell out to `open` to see the rendered image. This is also the natural companion to Markdown docs that embed images: if I follow a link to the image file itself (rather than viewing it inline in the doc), I'd expect to see it rendered too. ### Additional context **Feasibility notes (from reading the open source):** I'm not a Warp engineer, so take this as an outside read rather than an authoritative scoping, but from browsing the public repo it looks like most of the pieces already exist: - File-type → viewer dispatch already has a well-defined seam. `OpenableFileType` (code file type detection) and `FileTarget` (which viewer to route to: `MarkdownViewer`, `CodeEditor`, external editor, etc.) live in `app/src/util/openable_file_type.rs`, resolved via `resolve_file_target_with_editor_choice`. There's already an `is_supported_image_file()` helper right next to it (covers jpg/jpeg/png/gif/webp/svg) — but nothing currently routes through it; SVG today falls into the generic `Text` bucket and opens in the code editor. - The image rendering machinery is already fully built and reused elsewhere. `warpui_core::elements::Image` (backed by `ImageCache`/`ImageType` in `crates/warpui_core/src/image_cache.rs`) already rasterizes SVGs via `usvg`/`resvg` (with a bundled fallback font) as well as PNG/JPEG/WebP/animated GIF via the `image` crate, with size-aware caching, fit modes (Cover/Contain/Stretch), and animation playback. - This machinery is already proven for exactly this use case: the Markdown viewer's inline image rendering (`crates/editor/src/render/element/image.rs`, `RenderableImage`) is a ~50-line wrapper that just constructs `Image::new(asset_source, CacheOption::BySize).contain()` and lets the cache/renderer do the work. - The panel-opening mechanism looks generic, not Markdown-specific. `open_file_notebook()` (`app/src/workspace/view.rs`) — which the `FileTarget::MarkdownViewer` case calls — just builds a `FilePane` and inserts it into the active pane group via the existing `Direction`/`PanesLayout` APIs. Nothing about it appears intrinsically tied to Markdown parsing. Net read: I didn't find an architectural blocker. It looks more like a composition gap — adding an `ImageViewer` (or similar) `FileTarget` variant, wiring `is_supported_image_file()` into the dispatch logic, and building a thin viewer pane that reuses the existing `Image` element and pane-opening plumbing the same way the Markdown viewer does. Happy to be corrected if there's context I'm missing from outside the public repo. Related: #13652 (markdown raw-HTML `` support). Possibly overlaps with #9729 (image preview in the Code Editor file pane + thumbnail strip) and #12093 (image previews in the code review panel) — those are scoped to inline/thumbnail previews within other panels rather than a standalone rendered viewer for an image file opened directly, but there may be shared plumbing worth coordinating on.

title: Markdown viewer: support common raw-HTML subset (img sizing, tables, details, anchors)
author: fbartho
body:
### Pre-submit Checks - [x] I have searched Warp feature requests and there are no duplicates - [x] I have searched Warp docs and my feature is not there ### Describe the solution you'd like? Warp's Markdown Viewer should support the common subset of raw inline HTML that real-world repo markdown relies on (this is standard practice under GitHub Flavored Markdown, and is what GitHub, GitLab, and most other markdown renderers already support). Today the viewer renders plain Markdown syntax reasonably well, but silently drops or ignores raw HTML tags, which breaks a lot of READMEs, docs, and notes that use HTML for layout control that Markdown syntax alone can't express. Prioritized list of tags, most important first: 1. **`` with `width` / `height` / `align`** — sizing control for images. This is the one that bit me directly (see repro below). 2. **`` and ``** — links, plus named anchors used as deep-link targets for hand-built tables of contents. 3. **Tables: `` / `` / `` / `` / `
` / ``** — needed for cells that require a line break (`
`) or a `colspan`/`rowspan`, which Markdown table syntax cannot express at all. 4. **`
` / ``** — collapsible sections, extremely common in READMEs, changelogs, and bug-report templates (including this repo's own issue templates elsewhere on GitHub). 5. **`
`** — explicit line breaks, most often needed inside table cells. 6. **``** — keyboard-shortcut styling. Feels especially on-brand for a terminal app's own markdown viewer. 7. **`` / ``** — subscript/superscript. 8. **`

` / `

`** — centered "hero" headers, very common at the top of README files. 9. **`` / ``** — light/dark logo variant swapping, standard pattern for README badges/logos. I'd be happy to see this land as "the common subset above," even if full arbitrary-HTML rendering (with all its sanitization complexity) is out of scope. ### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe. I'm always frustrated when I write locally-generated SVG dashboards into my Markdown notes and Warp's viewer won't let me size them. Concretely, today in Warp's Markdown Viewer (verified on this build): Works, but not usefully — renders the image at a small fixed width that ignores the SVG's intrinsic `width`/`height`, and does not scale when the pane or document is resized: ```markdown ![Pipeline Funnel](assets/chart.svg) ``` Does not render at all (expected: image displayed at 90% of the pane width): ```html ``` Since the Markdown-image renderer ignores the SVG's own intrinsic dimensions, the HTML `width` attribute is the only author-controllable sizing signal available — and Warp currently drops it, so there is no way to control image size at all short of re-encoding the SVG itself. Two more everyday examples that currently don't render, both very common in repo docs: ```html
Click to expand Hidden content here, only shown when expanded.
``` ```markdown Press Cmd+K to open the command palette. ``` ### Additional context - Motivating use case: I generate SVG dashboards locally (charts, funnels, etc.) and embed them in Markdown notes for later reference. Without HTML `width`/`height` support, there's no way to size these embedded charts sensibly inside the viewer. - This is a distinct request from "render a full embedded browser/webview" (e.g. #8548, #9194) — I'm not asking for arbitrary script execution or a browser engine, just for the Markdown renderer to handle the same safe, static HTML subset that GitHub's own Markdown renderer supports. - Environment: Warp `0.2026.07.01.09.21.01` on macOS `27.0`. ### Operating system (OS) macOS ### How important is this feature to you? 3

title: Can't use custom inference without Warp credits on Pro plan. No option to downgrade to free plan either.
author: anomit
body: ### Pre-submit Checks - [x] I have [searched Warp bugs](https://github.com/warpdotdev/warp/issues?q=is%3Aissue+label%3ABUG) and there are no duplicates - [x] I have [searched Warp known issues page](https://docs.warp.dev/help/known-issues) and my issue is not there - [ ] I have an issue with AI and have included the debugging ID (Optional, but helps expedite the AI quality fix). [Debugging ID instructions](https://docs.warp.dev/agent-platform/agent/ai-faqs#gathering-ai-debugging-id) - [ ] I have technical issue and have included the logs (optional, but helps expedite the bug fix). [Log instructions](https://docs.warp.dev/support-and-community/troubleshooting-and-support/sending-us-feedback#gathering-warp-logs) ### Describe the bug I have configured an OpenRouter API key as well as xAI integration. My Pro plan renewal expired. I have no way to downgrade to free plan. So now I am stuck with an inability to use the AI features. Furthermore, the only way I can select these custom models is with `/model` from the terminal. I have no way to set this from `Profiles` settings, whether on `default` or a new one that I try to create. I never get to see the custom models there. ### To reproduce 1. Be on any plan 2. Let the plan renewal be overdue 3. Add custom inference points 4. Be unable to use AI features on the terminal ### Expected behavior _No response_ ### Screenshots, videos, and logs ### Operating system (OS) macOS ### Operating system and version Tahoe Version 26.5.2 (25F84) ### Shell Version zsh 5.9 (arm64-apple-darwin25.0) ### Current Warp version v0.2026.07.08.17.54.stable_02 ### Regression No, this bug or issue has existed throughout my experience using Warp ### Recent working Warp date _No response_ ### Additional context _No response_ ### Does this block you from using Warp daily? Yes, this issue prevents me from using Warp daily. ### Is this an issue only in Warp? Yes, I confirmed that this only happens in Warp, not other terminals. ### Warp Internal (ignore): linear-label:b9d78064-c89e-4973-b153-5178a31ee54e None

title: macOS: Stuck in loop on TCC permission dialog for Documents folder — Allow does nothing or re-presents the dialog
author: fbartho
body: ## Describe the bug Warp got stuck asking for permission to access my **Documents** folder. The macOS TCC dialog says: > "Warp" would like to access files in your Documents folder. Clicking **Allow** either does nothing, or instantly re-presents the same permission dialog. The dialog cannot be dismissed by granting permission. Possibly relevant: shortly before (or overlapping with) this dialog, Warp presented a request to access my **Downloads** folder, which I approved successfully. The Documents request may have raced with / overlapped the Downloads one. ## To reproduce 1. Trigger Warp to access the Downloads folder (TCC prompt appears) and approve it. 2. Warp then (or concurrently) requests access to the Documents folder. 3. Click **Allow** on the Documents dialog. 4. Dialog re-appears immediately (or the click has no effect). ## Expected behavior Clicking Allow grants access and dismisses the dialog once. ## Screenshots Standard macOS folder-access permission dialog for Documents ## Environment - **Warp version:** v0.2026.07.01.09.21.stable_01 - **OS:** macOS 27.0 (build 26A5378j) - **Hardware:** Apple Silicon Mac

title: Requiring HTTPS for custom provider backend breaks local inference
author: tkuhlengel
body: ### Pre-submit Checks - [x] I have [searched Warp bugs](https://github.com/warpdotdev/warp/issues?q=is%3Aissue+label%3ABUG) and there are no duplicates - [x] I have [searched Warp known issues page](https://docs.warp.dev/help/known-issues) and my issue is not there - [ ] I have an issue with AI and have included the debugging ID (Optional, but helps expedite the AI quality fix). [Debugging ID instructions](https://docs.warp.dev/agent-platform/agent/ai-faqs#gathering-ai-debugging-id) - [ ] I have technical issue and have included the logs (optional, but helps expedite the bug fix). [Log instructions](https://docs.warp.dev/support-and-community/troubleshooting-and-support/sending-us-feedback#gathering-warp-logs) ### Describe the bug Requiring HTTPS on "bring your own AI" breaks any local services that do not have https enabled. The bug is that it is IMPOSSIBLE to save the endpoint url if it does not start with `https://`, when `http://` is more than sufficient for some users. ### To reproduce 1. Go to Settings 2. Click `+Add custom model` 3. Add an Endpoint name 4. **Add an Endpoint URL that starts with `http://`** 5. Fill in the rest of the items with appropriate information 6. Click save (you can't, it's blocked by the https filter) ### Expected behavior Allow users to save ANY valid http or https endpoint URL. ### Screenshots, videos, and logs * Not allowed: `http://` * Allowed: `https://` ### Operating system (OS) Linux ### Operating system and version 24.04 ### Shell Version _No response_ ### Current Warp version v0.2026.07.08.17.54.stable_02 ### Regression Yes, this bug started recently or with an X Warp version ### Recent working Warp date N/A ### Additional context The new "Bring your own AI" feature is not inclusive of actually bringing your own AI? ### Does this block you from using Warp daily? Yes, this issue prevents me from using Warp daily. ### Is this an issue only in Warp? Yes, I confirmed that this only happens in Warp, not other terminals. ### Warp Internal (ignore): linear-label:b9d78064-c89e-4973-b153-5178a31ee54e _No response_

title: InputBoxTypeSetting 'universal' from cloud sync corrupts terminal on Linux+Fish
author: hernanharco
body:
## Summary

Setting `input_box_type_setting` gets overwritten by cloud sync with the legacy value `"universal"` even when `is_settings_sync_enabled = false` (bug #6692). This causes `[ERROR] Failed to parse file value for setting InputBoxTypeSetting` followed by `Inhibiting writes for setting key terminal.input.input_box_type_setting`. After this, every command becomes corrupted: the agent setup script concatenates with the user command WITHOUT a separator.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Have a Warp Cloud account with `input_box_type_setting = "universal"` stored (legacy value from old versions)
2. Set `is_settings_sync_enabled = false` in `settings.toml`
3. Restart Warp on Linux with fish shell
4. Observe: `settings.toml` keeps getting overwritten with `is_settings_sync_enabled = true` and `input_box_type_setting = "universal"`
5. Every command fails with:

```
source '/tmp/.tmpXXXXX'cd /some/path
source: Error encountered while sourcing file '/tmp/.tmpXXXXXcd':
source: No existe el archivo o el directorio
```

## Actual behavior

- `input_box_type_setting` gets overwritten by cloud sync despite `is_settings_sync_enabled = false` (related to #6692)
- Logs show: `[ERROR] Failed to parse file value for setting InputBoxTypeSetting` and `Inhibiting writes for setting key terminal.input.input_box_type_setting`
- The agent setup script concatenates with the user command without a separator (missing `; ` or newline)
- Terminal becomes completely unusable — every command is corrupted
- Banner error: "Invalid value for 'input_box_type_setting'"

## Expected behavior

- `is_settings_sync_enabled = false` should completely disable cloud sync for ALL settings (fix for #6692)
- When a setting value is invalid, Warp should fall back gracefully without corrupting input
- Agent setup scripts should be properly separated from user commands (similar to PR #12473 fix)

## Environment

- **OS**: Linux (Ubuntu 24.04)
- **Warp version**: v0.2026.07.01.09.21.stable_01
- **Shell**: fish (via Homebrew)
- **Window system**: X11
- **Installation**: deb package

## Key logs

```
[ERROR] Failed to parse file value for setting InputBoxTypeSetting
[INFO] Inhibiting writes for setting key terminal.input.input_box_type_setting
[INFO] Updating local preference with storage key InputBoxTypeSetting and value "universal" to match cloud preference
[WARN] Error Failed to parse value for InputBoxTypeSetting comparing local value "Editor" for cloud preference
```

## Related

- #6692 — AI setting syncs even when Settings sync is disabled (OPEN)
- #5444 — Fish not working (OPEN)
- PR #12473 — Fixed similar concatenation bug for PATH capture (MERGED)

title: macOS accent popup inserts both the base letter and the selected accented character
author: ilya-sarantsev
body:
### Pre-submit Checks

- [x] I have [searched Warp bugs](https://github.com/warpdotdev/warp/issues?q=is%3Aissue+label%3ABUG) and there are no duplicates
- [x] I have [searched Warp known issues page](https://docs.warp.dev/help/known-issues) and my issue is not there
- [ ] I have an issue with AI and have included the debugging ID (Optional, but helps expedite the AI quality fix). [Debugging ID instructions](https://docs.warp.dev/agent-platform/agent/ai-faqs#gathering-ai-debugging-id)
- [ ] I have technical issue and have included the logs (optional, but helps expedite the bug fix). [Log instructions](https://docs.warp.dev/support-and-community/troubleshooting-and-support/sending-us-feedback#gathering-warp-logs)

### Describe the bug

When holding a key that has accented variants (e.g. `u`, `o`, `a`), Warp correctly
shows the macOS press-and-hold accent popup. However, after selecting an accented
character from the popup, Warp inserts BOTH the base character and the selected
accented character, instead of just the accented one.

Example: holding `o` and selecting `ö` from the popup results in `oö` being typed,
not just `ö`

### To reproduce

1. In a Warp prompt, press and hold a letter key that has accent variants (e.g. `o`)
2. Wait for the macOS accent popup to appear
3. Select an accented variant from the popup (click it or press its number key)
4. Observe the text that gets inserted

### Expected behavior

Only the selected accented character should be inserted (e.g. `ö`), overriding the
base character, matching the behavior in Terminal.app


### Screenshots, videos, and logs

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0428be8c-6556-40e5-8c58-331d07e6b648

### Operating system (OS)

macOS

### Operating system and version

26.6 Beta (25G5057c)

### Shell Version

zsh 5.9 (arm64-apple-darwin25.0)

### Current Warp version

v0.2026.07.08.17.54.stable_02

### Regression

No, this bug or issue has existed throughout my experience using Warp

### Recent working Warp date

_No response_

### Additional context

Might be related to #3191

macOS built-in Terminal.app is fine. Ghostty has another issue (https://github.com/ghostty-org/ghostty/discussions/10669)

### Does this block you from using Warp daily?

Yes, this issue prevents me from using Warp daily.

### Is this an issue only in Warp?

Yes, I confirmed that this only happens in Warp, not other terminals.

### Warp Internal (ignore): linear-label:b9d78064-c89e-4973-b153-5178a31ee54e

None

title: Add a `warp <path>` desktop launcher for directories and files
author: psh4607
body:
### Pre-submit Checks

- [x] I searched Warp feature requests and did not find an issue for this focused desktop-launcher workflow.
- [x] I searched the Warp documentation and did not find a supported `warp <path>` command.

### Describe the solution you'd like?

Add a small, editor-style desktop launcher command for opening local paths in Warp:

```shell
warp .
warp AGENTS.md
```

Expected behavior:

1. `warp <directory>` launches or focuses the stable Warp desktop app and opens a terminal tab whose working directory is that directory.
2. `warp <file>` launches or focuses Warp and opens the file in Warp's built-in editor/Markdown viewer.
3. Relative paths are resolved from the caller's current working directory.
4. `warp` with no path launches or focuses the desktop app.
5. The Preview channel can use a channel-specific name such as `warp-preview`, consistent with `oz-preview` and `warpctrl-preview`.

This should be a thin desktop-launcher surface, not a replacement for either existing CLI:

- `oz` remains the agent CLI.
- `warpctrl` remains the richer API for controlling a running Warp instance.

The underlying app capabilities already exist:

- directories can be opened with `warp://action/new_tab?path=...` or the OS file-open event;
- files can be opened with `warp://action/open_file_editor?path=...` (implemented by #10233 for #9561);
- `warpctrl file open <path>` can open a file when a controllable app instance is already running.

The requested command would make those capabilities easy to discover and use from shells and IDE integrated terminals, while also handling app startup and channel routing.

### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

Developer tools commonly expose a memorable path launcher such as `code .`. Warp currently requires platform-specific commands or a user-maintained wrapper:

```shell
open -a Warp .
open "warp://action/open_file_editor?path=/absolute/path/to/AGENTS.md"
```

That is harder to remember, differs by operating system, and requires callers to choose different mechanisms for files and directories. The CLI portion of #9561 was deliberately left out when its URI support was implemented, and a later comment on that issue specifically requested `warp .` from an IDE integrated terminal. This issue tracks that narrower follow-up rather than reopening the file URI design.

Related but distinct:

- #9561 / #10233: external file opening via URI, now complete
- #11545: broad `warpctrl` scripting and live-instance control
- #2933: duplicate-window bug in the current macOS `open -a Warp .` workaround

### Additional context

Locally verified on Warp `0.2026.07.08.17.54.02` for macOS. A shell wrapper around the existing OS open events works for both examples, which demonstrates the user flow, but shipping the launcher with Warp would make it portable, documented, channel-aware, and supportable.

### Operating system (OS)

Cross-platform

### How important is this feature to you?

4

title: URL linkifier hijacks www. inside absolute file paths — renders http:// mid-path, path becomes unclickable and uncopyable
author: mrlerch
body:
## Bug

When terminal output contains an **absolute POSIX file path whose directory name contains a web domain** (a very common layout for web developers: `~/Sites/www.example.com/...`), Warp's URL smart-linkifier fires on the `www.` in the middle of the path. It wins over file-path detection, visually injects `http://` into the middle of the rendered text, and splits the path into a useless web link plus fragments.

## Repro

1. Have a project under a domain-named folder, e.g. `/Users/me/Sites/www.proproductssales.com/html-phpstorm/`
2. Run anything that prints a full path in that tree, e.g.:
   ```
   echo "Report saved: /Users/me/Sites/www.proproductssales.com/html-phpstorm/docs/report.md"
   ```
3. Look at the rendered output.

## Actual

Rendered as:

```
Report saved: /Users/me/Sites/http://www.proproductssales.com/html-phpstorm/docs/report.md
```

- The `www.proproductssales.com/html-phpstorm/docs/report.md` portion becomes a **web** link to `http://www.proproductssales.com/...` (404s — it's a local folder name).
- The leading `/Users/me/Sites/` is orphaned plain text.
- Cmd-clicking cannot open the file; selecting/copying the rendered line yields the corrupted `http://`-injected string, so the path can't even be copy-pasted.
- `file://` URIs don't help: `file:///Users/me/Sites/www.example.com/...` — the linkifier STILL grabs the inner `www.` and splits the file URI.

## Expected

If a token parses as an absolute path (starts with `/` or `~/`, no scheme), path/file linkification should take precedence over bare-`www.` URL detection for the *interior* of that token — or at minimum the copied text should be the original bytes, never the linkifier's rewritten form.

## Impact

This affects every AI-agent workflow (Warp AI, Claude Code, etc.) where the agent prints file paths for the user to click — any project living in an Apache-style `~/Sites/www.<domain>/` folder produces permanently broken links. Current workaround is creating dot-free symlinks next to every domain-named project folder, which shouldn't be necessary.

## Environment

- Warp 0.2026.07.08.17.54.02 (stable)
- macOS 15.7.7 (Intel)

title: Agent-driven zsh commands can block on spelling-correction prompts when correction is enabled
author: bivo
body: ### Pre-submit Checks - [x] I have [searched Warp bugs](https://github.com/warpdotdev/warp/issues?q=is%3Aissue+label%3ABUG) and there are no duplicates - [x] I have [searched Warp known issues page](https://docs.warp.dev/help/known-issues) and my issue is not there - [ ] I have an issue with AI and have included the debugging ID (Optional, but helps expedite the AI quality fix). [Debugging ID instructions](https://docs.warp.dev/agent-platform/agent/ai-faqs#gathering-ai-debugging-id) - [ ] I have technical issue and have included the logs (optional, but helps expedite the bug fix). [Log instructions](https://docs.warp.dev/support-and-community/troubleshooting-and-support/sending-us-feedback#gathering-warp-logs) ### Describe the bug When Warp Agent Mode runs commands in an interactive zsh session with spelling correction enabled, mistyped commands can trigger an interactive correction prompt like: ``` zsh: correct 'zef' to 'zed' [nyae]? ``` That prompt can block the agent flow and prevent the command from completing autonomously. ### To reproduce 1. Enable zsh correction in your shell config. - Example: set ENABLE_CORRECTION="true" in .zshrc 1. Open Warp. 1. Start an agent task that runs a mistyped shell command in the live terminal session. 1. Have the agent execute something like: ``` zef file.md ``` ### Expected behavior Agent-driven commands should not get stuck on zsh spelling-correction prompts. The command should either: - run as typed and fail normally, or - be executed in a way that suppresses zsh correction only for agent-driven commands Human-typed commands in the terminal should keep their normal shell behavior. ### Screenshots, videos, and logs ### Operating system (OS) macOS ### Operating system and version 26.5.2 ### Shell Version 5.9 (arm64-apple-darwin25.0) ### Current Warp version v0.2026.07.08.17.54.stable_02 ### Regression No, this bug or issue has existed throughout my experience using Warp ### Recent working Warp date _No response_ ### Additional context ### Why This Matters This breaks autonomous agent workflows in otherwise normal zsh setups. Users who have shell correction enabled are likely to hit this during command execution, validation, or follow-up commands such as formatting and tests. ### Notes This appears to be a bug in how agent-driven commands are submitted into interactive zsh sessions, rather than a problem with Warp’s built-in command-correction UI. A targeted fix would be to suppress zsh correction for **agent-driven commands only**, without changing behavior for human input. I already have a candidate fix prepared and can open a PR once this issue is filed and labeled appropriately (`ready-to-implement` if maintainers agree). ### Does this block you from using Warp daily? Yes, this issue prevents me from using Warp daily. ### Is this an issue only in Warp? Yes, I confirmed that this only happens in Warp, not other terminals. ### Warp Internal (ignore): linear-label:b9d78064-c89e-4973-b153-5178a31ee54e None

title: [Windows] Silent crash (Panic) on wake from sleep - wglSwapBuffers / wgpu-hal with Intel UHD 770
author: SMBarreraH
body:
### Pre-submit Checks

- [x] I have [searched Warp bugs](https://github.com/warpdotdev/warp/issues?q=is%3Aissue+label%3ABUG) and there are no duplicates
- [x] I have [searched Warp known issues page](https://docs.warp.dev/help/known-issues) and my issue is not there
- [ ] I have an issue with AI and have included the debugging ID (Optional, but helps expedite the AI quality fix). [Debugging ID instructions](https://docs.warp.dev/agent-platform/agent/ai-faqs#gathering-ai-debugging-id)
- [x] I have technical issue and have included the logs (optional, but helps expedite the bug fix). [Log instructions](https://docs.warp.dev/support-and-community/troubleshooting-and-support/sending-us-feedback#gathering-warp-logs)

### Describe the bug

When leaving the PC in sleep mode for an extended period, Warp closes silently. Upon reopening, it fails to restore the current session and instead loads an old, cached session state in a loop. Processes running in the background (like npm or dotnet) are left orphaned.

Based on the local warp.log files, the application is suffering a panic related to GPU context loss when waking up from sleep, specifically failing on wglSwapBuffers while using the OpenGL backend.

[ERROR] thread 'main' panicked at 'called `Result::unwrap()` on an `Err` value: Error { code: HRESULT(0x00000000), message: "La operación se completó correctamente." }': C:\cargo\registry\src\index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f\wgpu-hal-29.0.1\src\gles\device.rs:758
  28: DispatchMessageW
  29: CallWindowProcW
  30: wglSwapBuffers

[warp-logs.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29925445/warp-logs.zip)

### To reproduce

Environment:
- OS: Windows 11
- GPU: Intel(R) UHD Graphics 770
- Renderer: Gl (OpenGL)

1. Open Warp and start some background processes.
2. Put the Windows machine to sleep for a long period.
3. Wake the machine. Warp has crashed silently in the background.
4. Reopen Warp -> It restores an outdated session state instead of the one prior to the sleep state, and ports remain blocked by orphaned processes.

[ERROR] thread 'main' panicked at 'called `Result::unwrap()` on an `Err` value: Error { code: HRESULT(0x00000000), message: "La operación se completó correctamente." }': C:\cargo\registry\src\index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f\wgpu-hal-29.0.1\src\gles\device.rs:758
  28: DispatchMessageW
  29: CallWindowProcW
  30: wglSwapBuffers

### Expected behavior

When the PC wakes up from sleep mode, Warp should gracefully handle the GPU context loss (e.g., by recreating the swapchain/surface) and remain open and responsive.

Existing background processes (like npm or dotnet) running inside the terminal should remain attached and continue their execution normally. Furthermore, the terminal's session state should not become corrupted or frozen in a loop from a previous date.

### Screenshots, videos, and logs

[ERROR] thread 'main' panicked at 'called Result::unwrap() on an Err value: Error { code: HRESULT(0x00000000), message: "La operación se completó correctamente." }': C:\cargo\registry\src\index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f\wgpu-hal-29.0.1\src\gles\device.rs:758
28: DispatchMessageW
29: CallWindowProcW
30: wglSwapBuffers

[warp-logs.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29925448/warp-logs.zip)

### Operating system (OS)

Windows

### Operating system and version

Windows 11

### Shell Version

_No response_

### Current Warp version

v0.2026.07.08.17.54.stable_02

### Regression

Yes, this bug started recently or with an X Warp version

### Recent working Warp date

_No response_

### Additional context

- GPU: Intel(R) UHD Graphics 770
- Renderer: Gl (OpenGL)

### Does this block you from using Warp daily?

No

### Is this an issue only in Warp?

Yes, I confirmed that this only happens in Warp, not other terminals.

### Warp Internal (ignore): linear-label:b9d78064-c89e-4973-b153-5178a31ee54e

None

title: Feature: Show SSH/Warpified-subshell status on Agent Mode toolbelt
author: Bulzi-Robb
body:
### Describe the solution you'd like?

When using **Agent Mode** inside a **Warpified SSH session** or other **Warpified subshell** (Docker, nested shell, etc.), show a clear, persistent indicator on the **Agent Mode toolbelt / agent utility bar** that the active session is remote or nested.

Suggested UX (not prescriptive):
- A chip/badge on the Agent toolbelt such as `SSH · user@host` or `Subshell · docker`
- Optional hover/detail: hostname, user, remote cwd, warpify state (Warpified vs plain)
- Distinct from tab title alone so it remains visible while focused on the agent conversation UI
- Ideally works for:
  - Warp SSH wrapper sessions
  - Warpified `command ssh` / subshell sessions
  - Nested remote contexts where possible (documented limitations OK)

This should make it immediately obvious that agent-run commands execute on a remote/nested host rather than the local machine.

### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

While in Agent Mode over SSH/WSL-style nested sessions, it is easy to lose track of *where* the agent is executing. Vertical tabs can show working directory / metadata, and prompts can encode host info, but:

1. That context is easy to miss when attention is on the agent conversation and toolbelt.
2. Native agent filesystem tools are limited over SSH (fallback to shell), so misreading “local vs remote” has real impact on trust and safety of approvals.
3. Tab renames and prompt customization help, but are manual and not a first-class session-state signal on the Agent toolbelt.

I looked for an Agent toolbelt chip that indicates “SSH subshell / remote session” and did not find one in product docs or current UI.

### Related existing issues

- Closely related: #13430 — “Show SSH remote metadata in the terminal footer/toolbelt”
  - That request focuses on a persistent chip near terminal input for remote IP/host/env.
  - Discussion there notes hesitation about another footer conflicting with Agent sessions over SSH.
  - This request is complementary: put the remote/subshell signal **on the Agent Mode toolbelt itself**, where Agent Mode users already look for session controls.
- Related context/limitations: #6831 (AI/codebase features over SSH), SSH feature-support docs (native FS tools unavailable remotely; Agent falls back to terminal commands).

### Additional context

**Environment**
- Warp on Windows host with WSL2 Ubuntu sessions; frequent SSH / Warpify subshell workflows
- Heavy use of Agent Mode for coding tasks across local WSL and remote hosts

**Why toolbelt (not only prompt/tab)**
- Prompt customization and vertical-tab metadata are useful but easy to overlook during agent turns, approvals, and multi-pane agent work.
- Agent Mode already has a dedicated utility/toolbelt surface for session-related actions; remote/subshell state fits that model.

**Acceptance ideas**
- [ ] When the active pane is a Warpified SSH/subshell session and Agent Mode is active, the toolbelt shows a remote/subshell indicator without manual tab rename.
- [ ] Indicator updates or clears when leaving the remote/subshell session.
- [ ] Hover or expansion reveals enough identity (host/user/cwd) to avoid host mix-ups.
- [ ] Behavior is documented alongside SSH/subshell Warpify docs.

Thanks!

title: Classic completions remain stale after Tab then Backspace
author: maxmilian
body:
### Summary

With **Classic Completions** enabled, pressing <kbd>Tab</kbd> to open the completion menu and then <kbd>Backspace</kbd> back past the point where completion started leaves the old suggestion list on screen. The result set is stale — it no longer reflects what's actually in the input — and if you backspace all the way to an empty prefix, the entire original result set is re-shown.

This is called out directly in the source with a `TODO` in `app/src/terminal/input.rs` (the `update_tab_completion_menu` staleness check), which notes that for classic completions "if you hit tab and backspace, the result set won't go away (stale)."

### Steps to reproduce

1. Enable Classic Completions (`terminal.input.classic_completions_mode`).
2. In a directory that has, e.g., `Documents/` and `Downloads/`, type:
   ```
   cd Do
   ```
3. Press <kbd>Tab</kbd> — the classic completion menu opens (and the buffer is rewritten to a candidate, e.g. `cd Documents/`).
4. Press <kbd>Backspace</kbd> repeatedly until the input is back to `cd ` (i.e. past the point the completion was anchored to).

### Observed

The completion menu stays open showing the old candidates, even though the query they were computed for no longer matches the input. Backspacing to an empty prefix re-shows the whole original result set.

### Expected

Once the user edits the input back past the boundary the completion was based on (or to something incompatible with the original query), the stale suggestions should be cleared and the menu should close — the same way non-classic completions already invalidate on such edits. Continuous <kbd>Tab</kbd> cycling should remain unaffected.

### Environment

- WarpOss, built from `master` with the classic-completions feature enabled.
- macOS (Apple Silicon).

_Visual evidence (before/after) will be attached below._

title: Unable resume it stops imadatly have credits no error
author: FreddeITsupport98
body:
### Pre-submit Checks

- [x] I have [searched Warp bugs](https://github.com/warpdotdev/warp/issues?q=is%3Aissue+label%3ABUG) and there are no duplicates
- [x] I have [searched Warp known issues page](https://docs.warp.dev/help/known-issues) and my issue is not there
- [x] I have an issue with AI and have included the debugging ID (Optional, but helps expedite the AI quality fix). [Debugging ID instructions](https://docs.warp.dev/agent-platform/agent/ai-faqs#gathering-ai-debugging-id)
- [ ] I have technical issue and have included the logs (optional, but helps expedite the bug fix). [Log instructions](https://docs.warp.dev/support-and-community/troubleshooting-and-support/sending-us-feedback#gathering-warp-logs)

### Describe the bug

Problem: Warp AI Agent Stuck in Repetitive "Resume" Loop – Fails to Complete File Edit Task
Environment:
Warp AI agent session where the agent has access to file tools and is working inside a project folder.
Task given to the agent:
Update a README.md file by adding specific content (explicit warnings and usage disclaimers).
Observed behavior:

The agent correctly understands the request and begins planning the update.
Instead of editing the file or producing the updated content, the agent enters a loop:
It repeatedly adds new "resume" blocks in the conversation.
Each resume cycle re-states similar reasoning about reading the README and making the update.
The agent never calls a file edit tool and never outputs the actual changes.

The user has to manually type “resume” multiple times, but the agent continues generating new resume/thinking cycles without making progress.
The task is never completed — no file is modified and no final updated content is shown.

Expected behavior:
The agent should read the current README.md, apply the requested changes, and either show the diff, output the new content, or confirm that the file was successfully updated.
Actual behavior:
The agent gets stuck in an internal reasoning/resume loop. It acknowledges the task but fails to transition into actual tool use or produce any output. The loop continues indefinitely.
Impact:
Any task that requires the agent to perform file edits or multi-step modifications after reasoning becomes unusable. The user is forced into a manual “resume” loop with zero progress.
Screen recording attached:
19-second recording showing the full loop in the Warp session.

### To reproduce

Here's a clean reproduction-focused description you can use:

Reproduction Steps – Warp AI Agent Stuck in "Resume" Loop
Problem:
When asked to update a README.md file, the Warp AI agent enters a repetitive “resume” loop and never performs the actual file edit or produces the updated content.
Steps to Reproduce:

Open a Warp AI agent session in a project folder that contains a README.md file.
Give the agent the following type of task:
“Update the README.md with explicit warnings and usage disclaimers.”
The agent starts reasoning about the task and says it will read the README and make the changes.
Instead of editing the file, the agent begins adding multiple “resume” blocks.
Each new resume cycle repeats similar reasoning (planning to read the file and update it) but never executes a file edit.
Manually type resume in the chat.
→ The agent generates another resume/thinking block instead of completing the task.
Repeat typing resume. The agent continues looping without ever calling a file modification tool or showing the updated README content.

### Expected behavior

Expected behavior:
The agent should read the current README.md, apply the requested changes, and either:

Show the diff / updated content, or
Confirm that the file was successfully edited.

Actual behavior:
The agent gets stuck in a reasoning loop. It keeps generating new “resume” entries with almost identical thinking but never transitions to tool execution or final output. The file remains unchanged.
Result:
The task cannot be completed. The agent requires constant manual “resume” prompts but makes no progress on the actual file update.
Screen recording:
Attached 19-second recording showing the full loop from the moment the task is given until multiple resume cycles have occurred.

### Screenshots, videos, and logs

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1fdecab2-e56b-450c-9745-cfa881de5a28

### Operating system (OS)

Windows

### Operating system and version

win 11

### Shell Version

cmd or powershell

### Current Warp version

leatest

### Regression

No, this bug or issue has existed throughout my experience using Warp

### Recent working Warp date

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

Yes, this issue prevents me from using Warp daily.

### Is this an issue only in Warp?

title: Distro/OS logo in the system prompt
author: IGLADI
body: ### Pre-submit Checks - [x] I have [searched Warp feature requests](https://github.com/warpdotdev/warp/issues?q=is%3Aissue+label%3Aenhancement) and there are no duplicates - [x] I have [searched Warp docs](https://docs.warp.dev) and my feature is not there ### Describe the solution you'd like? Hey, first of all, thank you for all the work you've put into Warp. I really love the product, and now that it's open source, I can finally recommend it to my colleagues. I'm still looking forward to the VS Code integration, though. I'm here with another, much simpler feature request: In my Zsh prompt, I display the logo of the operating system (or Linux distribution). It's really useful because I SSH into many different systems, and it lets me identify them at a glance. It would be great if Warp's system prompt could do this. Here's an example (you can thus see I'm on fedora): ### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe. _No response_ ### Additional context _No response_ ### Operating system (OS) Linux ### How important is this feature to you? 2 ### Warp Internal (ignore) - linear-label:39cc6478-1249-4ee7-950b-c428edfeecd1 None
